Aurangabad group of caves, lying at a height of nearly 70 ft from the surrounding ground level, is hewn out of the Deccan Trap formation. Dedicated to Buddhism, there are 12 caves in all, which fall into three separate groups. The first group consists of caves number 1 to 5, the second from 6 to 9, and the third from 10 to 12. These caves are datable from around the 3rd century AD to 7th century AD. So, I hadn't even known about uh these caves, but uh they are similar to the ones at Ellora and Ajanta.
